nodes.label: AI as administrative efficiency tool
nodes.grounding_span: I use it mostly for administrative tasks

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: More likely to collaborate. Occasionally I will delegate though.

nodes.label: AI reliability and consistency
nodes.grounding_span: AI was refusing to write the 1:1 report that lesson, despite being given the exact same prompt

nodes.label: AI as content creator for lessons
nodes.grounding_span: I also occasionally use AI to create lesson content, such as maths questions

nodes.label: AI in education: social irreplaceability
nodes.grounding_span: part of school is the social aspect. While AI may be able to teach maths, it can't teach how to socialise.

nodes.label: Productivity and time efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I find it quicker and easier to do it myself

nodes.label: Professional autonomy and control
nodes.grounding_span: I think it would take too long to get an AI model to understand which branding to use

nodes.label: Social development in education
nodes.grounding_span: it can't teach how to socialise

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I anticipate on using it more and more in the workplace

nodes.label: Preference for manual handling of branded tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I find it quicker and easier to do it myself

nodes.label: Task-specific delegation based on formatting complexity
nodes.grounding_span: I think it would take too long for AI to format the task in the way I would need

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement
nodes.grounding_span: I would say 'homework completion should be the main area to work on'
